The result is visible now; people simply dump their waste on the street, causing extreme nuisance.

216058.explanation: The proposal may lead to easing and prolongation of benefit dependency. Policy must remain focused on independence.

216059.explanation: The waiting period encourages young people to first take responsibility through work, education, or their own network and prevents immediate entry into welfare.

216061.explanation: Permits should be handled not only cheaper but also faster and more cooperatively. Less bureaucracy strengthens entrepreneurship and leads to more investments and jobs in Velsen.

216062.explanation: Joint investments by companies strengthen business parks and the local economy. Municipal support is justified when there is co-financing and it leads to concrete improvements in accessibility, safety, or appearance.

216063.explanation: Accessibility must be practical and proportional. Mandatory adaptation of all facilities, regardless of function or use, leads to inefficient and symbolic policy.

216064.explanation: Refugees should not receive integration or language lessons, but guidance for return to their own home country. The government should facilitate return, not normalize permanent settlement.

216007.explanation: If property tax is lowered, various provisions such as road maintenance, sports and culture, as well as care for the vulnerable, must also be reduced or stopped.

216008.explanation: The revenue from the dog tax is spent on countering dog nuisance, enforcement, and collection costs. Exemption is possible for low-income people. ChristenUnie supports a gradual reduction of the dog tax.

216009.explanation: City marketing Velsen is supported by the municipality and does good work bringing together clubs, businesses, and organizations in various activities. Entrepreneurs that fit Velsen can find it well through this.

216010.explanation: The municipality strives to obtain additional resources from the national or provincial government for generating sustainable energy. In addition, the municipality can also offer practical help, for example by making roofs available.

216011.explanation: ChristenUnie also sees that giving priority to refugees means fewer rental homes are available. Nevertheless, ChristenUnie chooses to give priority to meet national and provincial housing requirements and to better distribute status holders across the municipality.

216012.explanation: With extra funds, newcomers can be well guided to find a good place in society.

216013.explanation: With a safe asylum seekers' center (AZC) with all necessary facilities, people who have fled can be properly and responsibly accommodated, allowing goodbye to the expensive asylum ship shelter in Velsen-Noord.

216016.explanation: It is important that green maintenance is done professionally. Therefore, it is not left to residents unless they organize professionally and make use of the right to challenge.

216017.explanation: Children from low-income families can participate in sports or arts and culture through the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture. There is also a scheme for adults for socio-cultural activities.

216020.explanation: ChristenUnie wants sufficient availability and visibility of neighborhood officers and enforcement officers (BOAs).

216021.explanation: Camera surveillance can be used temporarily if there is a concrete threat, but not to continuously monitor residents and thus infringe on privacy.

216025.explanation: To restore a healthy housing mix in IJmuiden and Velsen-Noord, no more social rental homes will be built there. New social rental homes will be built in the other village centers.

216026.explanation: The number of social rental homes in IJmuiden and Velsen-Noord remains the same. Existing social rental homes can be replaced by new ones (which will usually be more expensive and much better). Adding new, more expensive homes improves the housing mix.

216027.explanation: This is a measure to prevent speculation and extremely high rents for new build homes. In exceptional circumstances or if an affordable rent is requested, the owner-occupancy obligation can be waived.

216028.explanation: Where necessary, we transform vacant office buildings into homes, with attention to a good balance between living, working, recreation, green spaces, and shopping. We ensure enough office space remains available.

216030.explanation: ChristenUnie is committed to supporting vulnerable residents of Velsen as well as possible. Budget cuts in this area increase problems such as crime, domestic violence, and mental health issues-and the related costs.

216031.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that people who provide informal care make an important contribution to society. Therefore, they do not have to apply for jobs.

216032.explanation: Youth are the future. Easily accessible youth workers are very important to support young people. You can approach them with problems and they help find solutions. This prevents costly youth care.

216033.explanation: In new construction, we want extra parking spaces under the homes or underground. Space is limited, so we must become less dependent on private cars by good public transport, nearby facilities, good walking and cycling routes, and shared transport.

216034.explanation: Space is scarce. We want green areas and we want to build. Therefore, we must become less dependent on private cars by good public transport, nearby facilities, good walking and cycling routes, and shared transport. This can prevent parking permits from becoming necessary.

216035.explanation: For traffic safety, the maximum speed within built-up areas will be 30 kilometers per hour. Only on roads with public transport and connecting roads does 50 kilometers per hour apply.

216036.explanation: We support community centers where people meet for a listening ear. Club life is important for social cohesion; it offers space to eat together, relax together, learn, or help each other.

216038.explanation: In Velsen, ChristenUnie wants to improve youth participation. The decisions we make now are about their future. Therefore, ChristenUnie supports a youth council that advises both on request and unsolicited and has a clear mandate.

216039.explanation: Transport poverty can be tackled with free bus transport, but ChristenUnie prefers to address poverty holistically to ensure people can afford what they need.

216040.explanation: The municipality must urge the government to accelerate replacement of the most polluting installations with new clean ones. The government should support this if necessary.

216044.explanation: The municipality can contribute by selling land at a lower price with a good program of requirements so that housing corporations and/or care organizations can realize housing and access to care.

216045.explanation: The municipality must add trees and greenery so that from every house there is visibility of at least three trees, 30% leaf cover in the neighborhood, and a green space within 300 meters.

216046.explanation: Relationship problems are at the root of many social issues, such as youths with problems, loneliness, mental health issues, and housing shortages due to many divorces. Relationship courses can help reduce these problems.

216048.explanation: There is already a free subscription for everyone up to 28 years old; it would be nice to add a cheap starter subscription for all ages. Subscriptions are only a small part of libraries' income. However, ChristenUnie does not find it necessary to make it entirely free for everyone.

216049.explanation: ChristenUnie wants specific support for young caregivers, especially those who have to combine caregiving tasks with school or study. The municipality will enter into dialogue with these youths to hear what they need.

216051.explanation: Where necessary, we convert vacant shops into homes to keep the city center livable and vibrant, with attention to a good balance between living, working, recreation, green, and shopping.

216052.explanation: ChristenUnie wants the municipality to provide extra insulation subsidies to people with owner-occupied homes and low income, based on income-dependent subsidies or a sustainability loan.

216053.explanation: ChristenUnie wants to add a bus stop between Heerenduinen Swimming Pool and the Heerenduinen entrance. Both locations receive many visitors, and parking lots are often full. Accessibility via public transport for both locations should be improved.

216054.explanation: Get rid of it. Harmful. ChristenUnie wants gambling and alcohol advertisements banned from public spaces, bus shelters, and other (light) advertising columns.

216055.explanation: To prevent flooding, we provide water retention and other climate-adaptive measures. By combining these with nature development, we create a pleasant environment for people, animals, plants, and trees.

216056.explanation: Space is scarce. We want green and we want to build. Therefore, parking space must come underground or under homes. Shared cars can also reduce the number of parked cars. Paid parking can be a last necessary resort.

216057.explanation: Separating waste well must pay off. Therefore, in Velsen we already pay depending on how much residual waste we deliver.

216058.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that when people live together, this has consequences for their benefits. The municipality applies a personal approach to determine what is reasonable in the situation and for how long.

216059.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that young people should be encouraged to find work or education in a timely manner. Ending up young in social assistance is usually not a good sign, but tailored solutions must be possible.

216061.explanation: ChristenUnie believes that the costs for assessing a permit application should be charged by the municipality. If it is possible to simplify the process, this can naturally reduce the costs.

216062.explanation: If companies want to improve their own sites, the municipality does not need to contribute. However, the municipality can make the public space around company sites more attractive by adding green areas where people can exercise and walk.

216063.explanation: A new Sports Facility Policy will be introduced to provide clear prospects for sports clubs. All sports facilities must be accessible to people with disabilities so that they too can be active and participate in sports clubs.

216064.explanation: Much is already done by the library and volunteers regarding language lessons for refugees. ChristenUnie thinks it is important that refugees can integrate into society as quickly as possible. This requires broad involvement from the government, the neighborhood, entrepreneurs, and companies.

216007.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes that the amount of property tax (OZB) can be lowered if the municipality of Velsen works to reduce the WOZ value. This leads to tax benefits for residents concerning income tax, water board tax, etc. This does not necessarily have negative consequences for the municipality.

216008.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes that the dog tax, as a targeted tax, should actually be used for provisions such as off-leash areas and poop bags. This reduces the nuisance of stepping in dog waste, cycling through it, or getting your wheelchair dirty up to your elbows.

216009.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes the municipality does not need to promote itself if it operates from an inviting business climate; the municipality promotes itself naturally in that way. Entrepreneurs themselves also have an important role in this.

216010.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes the municipality should inform residents about and facilitate the application for the many subsidies available. This should be done in consultation with and support from Energiek Velsen, Klimaatroute, and energy coaches.

216011.explanation: From the principles Liberaal Blauw applies to refugee policy, refugees can register for housing in a regular way from a transitional location.

216012.explanation: The municipality does not need to spend more money because sufficient funding streams are already available. At the transitional location proposed by Liberaal Blauw, responsible work on integration and education can take place.

216013.explanation: Liberaal Blauw opposes an asylum seekers center (AZC) in a vulnerable municipality like Velsen and advocates for regional transition centers and shelter closer to the country of origin. An AZC puts too much pressure on Velsen, which is vulnerable and already houses thousands of (labor) migrants and refugees (including Ukrainians).

216016.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes this can precisely be an initiative from the village and neighborhood budget. Residents who have a plan to improve their neighborhood should also receive funding from the municipality in the future.

216017.explanation: Residents receiving social assistance can already make use of the Youth Fund for Sports and Culture.

216020.explanation: Liberaal Blauw observes that there are insufficient enforcement officers (BOA's) in the municipality of Velsen and too little enforcement takes place (drug nuisance, improper use of housing, traffic bottlenecks/parking, etc.).

216021.explanation: In places where there is a lot of nuisance, it is very desirable. Additionally, from Liberaal Blauw's perspective, placing cameras has a preventive effect.

216025.explanation: This can lead to a fair distribution of social housing. Liberaal Blauw notes that the planning in Velserbroek is already more than sufficient and that building must also contribute to good flow-through, preservation of livability, and support.

216026.explanation: Liberaal Blauw wants to ensure a fair distribution of social rental housing to achieve a varied housing supply in the neighborhoods and centers of Velsen.

216027.explanation: This can only be done with new construction if the municipality contributes land, but not with existing properties. Liberaal Blauw believes everyone should be free to sell their home to whomever they want. Legislation regarding rental income has since been adjusted, and exploitative landlords have already dropped out.

216028.explanation: Liberaal Blauw notes that there are hardly any vacant office buildings in the municipality of Velsen and that the municipality itself does not own office buildings, let alone empty ones.

216030.explanation: Liberaal Blauw respects the recently adopted decisions regarding the Poverty Vision.

216031.explanation: Liberaal Blauw has much respect for caregivers and finds the caregiver appreciation appropriate.

216032.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes youth work has a preventive effect on the attitudes and actions of young people. Budget cuts on youth work are unacceptable to Liberaal Blauw. Youth already face social pressure and limited recreational opportunities.

216033.explanation: Liberaal Blauw wants parking spaces at the edges of neighborhoods. We want to start a pilot to introduce supervised parking so that work vans and trucks can safely park outside residential areas, benefiting safety and livability within neighborhoods.

216034.explanation: Liberaal Blauw supports free parking. Paid parking and parking permits lead to the “Olive Patch Effect.”

216035.explanation: Liberaal Blauw thinks this is a good plan and wants the design of public spaces to align with it. Besides the 30 km/h speed limit inside built-up areas, a 60 km/h limit could be introduced between the centers, outside the built-up areas in Velsen. This would allow for fewer traffic signs.

216036.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes this should depend on the number of residents and geographic location. A proper balance must be sought.

216038.explanation: Liberaal Blauw is a strong supporter of this. Both solicited and unsolicited advice are appreciated to develop better youth policies. Those who have the youth have the future. Moreover, Liberaal Blauw also wishes to appoint a child mayor.

216039.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes people should be able to make their own choices in this and bear personal responsibility. There are, incidentally, national regulations and resources for this.

216040.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes good agreements have already been made regarding the sustainability of the steel industry and that Tata Steel should be given the chance to implement them.

216044.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes this is already arranged and that WMO budgets and personal budgets (PGB’s) can be used to purchase care. Cooperation with housing corporations is also used to implement this, including to promote flow-through.

216045.explanation: After all, a tree policy plan has been developed for this. It also limits the nuisance caused by high water levels.

216046.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes residents have personal responsibility in this.

216048.explanation: Liberaal Blauw is in favor of giving residents up to the age of 16 a free library subscription, but not all residents.

216049.explanation: Even though they have personal responsibility in this.

216051.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes the designation of many buildings can be changed from retail/mixed use to residential. It is then up to the owners to cooperate.

216052.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes the municipality should inform residents about and facilitate applications for the many subsidies available to insulate homes. This should be done in consultation with and supported by Klimaatroute, Energiek Velsen, and energy coaches.

216053.explanation: Liberaal Blauw finds it valuable to allow residents, especially students, to make use of the swimming pool.

216054.explanation: Liberaal Blauw follows here our former mayor and ex-minister Franc Weerwind. Especially for underage residents who should not legally be confronted with this, it applies. You can hardly send them onto the road blindfolded.

216055.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes we must do this together. Arrangements and subsidies can become available to residents if the municipality works constructively with the Hoogheemraadschappen Rijnland and Hollands Noorderkwartier.

216056.explanation: Liberaal Blauw supports free parking; paid parking and permits lead to spillover effects. The problem is merely shifted to the next street or neighborhood. Only for shops is a (Liberaal) Blue zone sufficient.

216057.explanation: We all pay the same. However, it is important to continue separating waste and to deliver residual waste separately. Everyone must also be able to do this. The national government will drastically raise the tax/charge on delivered residual waste! And we all pay that bill.

216058.explanation: This is neither controllable nor enforceable.

216059.explanation: This concerns a waiting time of only four weeks. What is the problem if it concerns an application and not an immediate benefit? Actively searching for work and/or education is not a punishment, is it?

216061.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes this also depends on the type of permit and the company involved. Tailor-made solutions are appropriate here.

216062.explanation: Liberaal Blauw welcomes the initiatives of GreenBiz and notes that the business community responds positively. We encourage the initiatives of GreenBiz, where businesses themselves implement plans.

216063.explanation: Liberaal Blauw finds this more than self-evident! This also applies to all public buildings in our municipality.

216064.explanation: Liberaal Blauw believes the national government, COA, and Refugee Organizations must play an important role here. This should be arranged in advance in transit centers where refugees can integrate and receive education.

New social housing should primarily be built in Santpoort-Noord, Santpoort-Zuid, Velserbroek, Velsen-Zuid and Driehuis.

All residents of Velsen should receive a free library membership.
The municipality should co-fund housing options for the elderly that allow them to live at home and have access to care.
statements.moreInfo: In a knarrenhofje elderly people live together in a small community. They help and care for each other so they can live independently for longer.

The municipality should abolish the dog tax.
The municipality should allow benefit recipients to live together on a six-month trial basis without this affecting their benefits.

The municipality should spend more money on integrating newcomers into society.
The municipality should make vacant retail premises suitable for residential use.
People receiving welfare benefits who provide informal care to a family member or acquaintance do not have to apply for jobs.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.
More parking spaces should be created in the neighborhoods.
The municipality should provide funding for residents who want to maintain the trees, plants and grass in their neighborhood themselves.
All sports facilities in Velsen should be made accessible for people with disabilities.
The municipality should cut spending on youth work.
The municipality should spend more on respite care to give young caregivers temporary relief.

The municipality should convert vacant office buildings for residential use.
No new social rental housing should be built in IJmuiden and Velsen-Noord.
